zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Brooke於大約1555年出生在英國倫敦時，她的父親John Brooke26歲，母親Elizabeth Whetman22歲。她於1576年6月18日與Robert Foote在英國米德萊可雷迪其聖 萊昂納達斯結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1609年2月15日之後在英國艾可沙爾福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Brooke于大约1555年出生在英国倫敦时，她的父亲John Brooke26岁，母亲Elizabeth Whetman22岁。她于1576年6月18日与Robert Foote在英国米德萊可雷迪其聖 萊昂納達斯结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1609年2月15日之后在英国艾可沙爾福德。

nameMeanings.name: Brooke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Brook  , which preserves a trace of the Old English dative singular case, originally used after a preposition (e.g. ‘at the brook’).

History: In 1650, Robert and Mary Mainwaring Brooke brought ten children and a number of servants with them from England to MD, where Robert became governor. Although the fourteen known contemporary Brooke immigrants in VA included Robert's brothers Richard and Humphrey, the relationships of the others are unknown. Brooke family memorials remain in the Anglican church at Whitchurch, Hampshire, England.

variants.name: Brook
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by a brook, stream, water-meadows or low, marshy ground, from Middle English brook, Old English brōc ‘brook, stream’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, such as Brook (Kent, Rutland) or Brooke (Norfolk).
North German: topographic name for someone who lived by a water meadow or marsh, from Low German brook (cognate with German Bruch ‘swamp’ and Old English brōc; see 1 above).
Americanized form of Dutch Broek: topographic name from broek, a cognate of 2 above.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Brooke was born about 1555, in London, England, her father, John Brooke, was 26 and her mother, Elizabeth Whetman, was 22. She married Robert Foote on 18 June 1576, in St Leonard's, Shoreditch, Middlesex, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 2 daughters. She died after 15 February 1609, in Shalford, Essex, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Brooke(이)가 대략 1555년 영국 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Brooke(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Whetman(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1576년 6월 18일 영국 미들레세스 쇼레딧치 스트 리나르드'스에서 Robert Foote(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1609년 2월 15일 이후 영국 에세스 샤퍼드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Brooke nasceu aproximadamente 1555, em London, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Brooke, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Whetman, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert Foote em 18 de junho de 1576, em St Leonard's, Shoreditch, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu após 15 de fevereiro de 1609, em Shalford, Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Brooke родилась около 1555 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, её отцу по имени John Brooke было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Whetman, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Foote 18 июня 1576 г. в местности ст леонард'с, шауредич, миддлесекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла после 15 февраль 1609 г. в местности шэлфорд, эссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Brooke nació aproximadamente 1555, en London, Inglaterra, su papá, John Brooke, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Whetman, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Robert Foote el 18 de junio de 1576, en St Leonard's, Shoreditch, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió después de 15 de febrero de 1609, en Shalford, Essex, Inglaterra.
